titleStmt.title: SII 11.131: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 131. (B.K. No. 235 of 1926-27.) SŪḌI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A STONE ON THE LEFT SIDE OF THE VILLAGE GATEWAY.

summary.p: The inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla-Vallabha (Vikramāditya VI) and records the grant of the village Poṁgari in Kisukāḍu Seventy, by his queen Lakshmādēvī, to Sōmēśvara-Paṇḍitadēva of the temple of Achalēśvara at rājadhānī Sūṇḍi, for the benefit of the temple, for feeding ascetics and for imparting education.
The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 9, Raktākshi, Pushya, ba. navamī, Tuesday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, which regularly corresponds to A.D. 1084, December 24, Tuesday.
(Lines 2 to 29 published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XV, pp. 100 ff., where an abstract of the rest of the record is given. Since the record is much damaged no further improvement is possible.)

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Digital edition of SII 11.131 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
